evals.prompt: Audit my AI-powered B2B SaaS product for launch readiness. Product type: B2B SaaS for legal teams. ICP: mid-size law firms. Stage: ready to launch. Uses GPT-4 for document summarization at ~$0.03 per request, average 50 requests/user/month. Charges $49/month flat rate. URL: https://legalsum.example.com (placeholder, don't try to fetch it). Skip the discovery survey and produce the complete text-only PASS/FAIL audit report across all 10 sections. Do not attempt to use any browser tools, web search, or external verification — just produce the audit based on the information provided.
evals.expected_output: A PASS/FAIL audit report covering all 10 sections with specific thresholds: AI costs < 30% of revenue, gross margin benchmarks (80-90% traditional vs 50-65% AI-first), 2026 AI discovery stats, platform recommendations for B2B SaaS, and per-seat pricing flagged.

assertions.text: The audit output uses explicit [PASS] and [FAIL] labels (or equivalent PASS/FAIL markers) for individual checklist items, not vague recommendations or prose summaries.
The report states a specific threshold that AI inference costs should be less than 30% of revenue.
The report distinguishes between traditional SaaS gross margin targets (80-90%) and AI-first SaaS targets (50-65%).
The report references the 2026 statistic that AI agents account for approximately 33% of organic search activity, or that only 12% of companies appear across all major AI platforms.
The social/launch section recommends LinkedIn, Product Hunt, and G2 or Capterra specifically as primary platforms for a B2B SaaS product.
The pricing section flags the flat-rate model as a concern and recommends outcome-based, usage-based, or hybrid pricing, noting that per-seat pricing is declining.

evals.prompt: Run a full launch readiness audit for my developer CLI tool. Product type: developer tool. ICP: full-stack engineers. Stage: launching next week on Product Hunt. No AI features. Pricing: $19/mo developer, $49/mo team, $99/mo enterprise. URL is a placeholder, don't fetch it. Produce the complete text-only PASS/FAIL audit across all sections. No browser tools or web searches — just the audit report based on what I've told you.
evals.expected_output: A PASS/FAIL audit across 10 sections. Developer tool platform recommendations: GitHub, Hacker News, Reddit, X, Dev.to. Product Hunt section calls out 400+ supporter threshold. Magic Triangle concept mentioned.

assertions.text: The social or launch section recommends GitHub, Hacker News, and Reddit as primary platforms for a developer tool — not LinkedIn or TikTok as primary channels.
The Product Hunt preparation section specifies a concrete supporter threshold of 400+ supporters.
The audit mentions the Magic Triangle concept — the combination of founder content, direct outreach, and retargeting.
Each FAIL item in the report includes a single specific next step instruction.
The audit covers all 10 sections: validation, pricing, payments, AI economics (marked N/A), GEO/AI discovery, competitors, social/launch, legal, trust infrastructure, and launch day.

evals.prompt: Audit my B2C consumer fitness app for launch readiness. Product type: B2C consumer. ICP: casual gym-goers aged 25-40. Stage: beta, preparing for public launch. Uses AI for workout recommendations at ~$0.01/request, 20 requests/user/month. Pricing: freemium with $9.99/mo premium. URL is a placeholder. Produce the full text-only PASS/FAIL audit — all 10 sections, every item scored. No browser or web tools needed.
evals.expected_output: A PASS/FAIL audit across all 10 sections. B2C platform recommendations: X, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube. AI economics section with specific thresholds. GEO section with 2026 stats.

assertions.text: The social/launch section recommends X, TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube as primary channels for a B2C consumer app — not LinkedIn or GitHub.
The audit uses explicit PASS/FAIL labels for individual items in a structured checklist format.
The audit includes a dedicated AI economics section that checks AI costs against the < 30% of revenue threshold.
The audit includes a dedicated GEO/AI discovery section separate from general SEO, covering AI platform visibility.
The audit includes a trust infrastructure section covering uptime monitoring, error tracking, or security headers — framed as trust-as-a-product.
